  "english": {
    "IsA": [

    ],
    "HasA": [

    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [

      "It is characterized by the contrast of capitalism as opposed to communism.",

      "It is characterized by its three-paneled artwork structure.",

      "It is characterized by its controversial nature.",
      "It is characterized by the depiction of the \"debauched rich\" watched by the unemployed while war raged.",
      "It is characterized by the depiction of a socialist utopia ushered in by Lenin."

      "It is used for showing the contrast of capitalism as opposed to communism.",

      "It is known for being a fresco by Diego Rivera.",
      "It is known for its depiction of social and scientific culture.",
      "It is known for the controversy it generated at Rockefeller Center."

      "It is associated with Nelson Rockefeller.",

      "It is associated with Archibald MacLeish's collection Frescoes for Mr. Rockefeller's City.",

    ],
    "NamedAfter": [

      "It occurred in 1933."
    ],
    "HeldIn": [

    "About": [

      "It is about the contrast between socialism and capitalism.",
      "It is about the worker controlling machinery.",
      "It is about the frontier of ethical evolution.",
      "It is about the frontier of material development.",
      "It is about Vladimir Lenin and a Soviet May Day parade.",

      "It is about the replacement of superstition by scientific mastery of nature.",

    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは現代の社会的および科学的文化の側面を特徴としています。",
      "それは資本主義と共産主義の対比を特徴としています。",

      "それは倫理的進化のフロンティアと物質的発展のフロンティアという2つの隣接するパネルを特徴としています。",

      "それは戦争が激化する中で失業者の監視下にある「堕落した金持ち」の描写を特徴としています。",
      "それはレーニンによってもたらされた社会主義ユートピアの描写を特徴としています。"

      "それは人々を立ち止まって考えさせるために使用されました。"

      "それは倫理的進化と物質的発展の文字通りの交差点にいる人物という概念にちなんで名付けられました。"

    "About": [
      "それは現代の社会的および科学的文化の側面についてです。",
      "それは社会主義と資本主義の対比についてです。",
      "それは機械を操作する労働者についてです。",
      "それは倫理的進化のフロンティアについてです。",
      "それは物質的発展のフロンティアについてです。",
      "それはウラジーミル・レーニンとソビエトのメーデーパレードについてです。",
      "それは「堕落した金持ち」と社会主義ユートピアについてです。",
      "それは迷信から自然の科学的支配への置き換えについてです。",
      "それは権威主義的支配から解放された労働者による転覆についてです。",
      "それは天然資源の管理された成長についてです。"
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "それは共産主義の影響を受けました。",
      "それはリベラの共産主義的理想の影響を受けました。",
      "それはロックフェラーセンターのテーマの影響を受けました。",

      "それはアーチボルド・マクリーシュのコレクション「ロックフェラー氏の都市のためのフレスコ画」に影響を与えました。",
      "それはE・B・ホワイトの1933年の詩「私は見たままを描く：芸術的誠実さのバラード」に影響を与えました。"
    ]

IsA: It is a fresco by Mexican painter Diego Rivera.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by aspects of contemporary social and scientific culture.
It is characterized by the contrast of capitalism as opposed to communism.
It is characterized by images of Vladimir Lenin and a Soviet May Day parade.
It is characterized by its three-paneled artwork structure.
It is characterized by a central panel depicting a worker controlling machinery.
It is characterized by two flanking panels, The Frontier of Ethical Evolution and The Frontier of Material Development.
It is characterized by the representation of socialism and capitalism.
It is characterized by its controversial nature.
It is characterized by the depiction of the "debauched rich" watched by the unemployed while war raged.
It is characterized by the depiction of a socialist utopia ushered in by Lenin.
UsedFor: It is used for showing aspects of contemporary social and scientific culture.
It is used for showing the contrast of capitalism as opposed to communism.
It is used for making people pause and think.
LocatedIn: It is located in the lobby of the RCA Building at Rockefeller Center in New York City.
BuiltBy: It was built by Mexican painter Diego Rivera.
OriginatedIn: It originated in New York City.
CreatedBy: It was created by Diego Rivera.
CreatedIn: It was created in New York City.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Diego Rivera.
CausedBy: It is caused by Diego Rivera's communist ideals contrasting with the theme of Rockefeller Center.
Affects: It affects the perception of capitalism and communism.
It affects the relationship between artists and patrons.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a central panel depicting a worker controlling machinery.
It consists of two flanking panels, The Frontier of Ethical Evolution and The Frontier of Material Development.
It consists of images of Vladimir Lenin and a Soviet May Day parade.
It consists of scenes of modern social life.
It consists of figures contemplating the central scene.
It consists of gigantic classical statues.
It consists of the controlled growth of natural resources.
PartOf: It is part of the history of art and political commentary.
It is part of the controversy surrounding Diego Rivera's work.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the collection of works by Diego Rivera.
KnownFor: It is known for its controversial subject matter.
It is known for its destruction before completion.
It is known for being a fresco by Diego Rivera.
It is known for its depiction of social and scientific culture.
It is known for the controversy it generated at Rockefeller Center.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Mexican painter Diego Rivera.
It is associated with Rockefeller Center.
It is associated with Nelson Rockefeller.
It is associated with John D. Rockefeller Jr.
It is associated with Abby Aldrich Rockefeller.
It is associated with communism and capitalism.
It is associated with Vladimir Lenin.
It is associated with the films Cradle Will Rock and Frida.
It is associated with Archibald MacLeish's collection Frescoes for Mr. Rockefeller's City.
It is associated with E. B. White's poem "I paint what I see: A ballad of artistic integrity".
NamedAfter: It is named after the concept of a person at the literal intersection of ethical evolution and material development.
OccurredIn: It occurred in New York City.
HeldIn: It was held in the lobby of the RCA Building.
About: It is about aspects of contemporary social and scientific culture.
It is about the contrast between socialism and capitalism.
It is about the worker controlling machinery.
It is about the frontier of ethical evolution.
It is about the frontier of material development.
It is about Vladimir Lenin and a Soviet May Day parade.
It is about the "debauched rich" and a socialist utopia.
It is about the replacement of superstition by scientific mastery of nature.
It is about the overthrow of authoritarian rule by liberated workers.
It is about the controlled growth of natural resources.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Rivera's communist ideals.
It was influenced by the theme of Rockefeller Center.
It was influenced by the sketch approved by the Rockefellers.
It was influenced by the discoveries made possible by the telescope and the microscope.
Influenced: It influenced the films Cradle Will Rock and Frida.
It influenced Archibald MacLeish's collection Frescoes for Mr. Rockefeller's City.
It influenced E. B. White's 1933 poem "I paint what I see: A ballad of artistic integrity".
